$comment: SMGP team-season history capsules, season 6 (SMGP-024). Base-universe history: the world's own arc, NOT the player career. DISPLAY-ONLY, never a fold input.

team.madonna.summary: Above the theatre, as ever, the king. While the whole grid shaved its wings and prayed in the corners, A. Senna sat in the mirrors of every wing-stripped hero and slingshotted past on the run to the line, the MADONNA 456 proving the tow bends to him too. A. Asselin closed the door with a butler's courtesy. The PALM 190 V10, no longer the paddock's loudest voice, remained its most punctual. When Monaco's finale stripped the slipstream away and handed the year's last word back to pure craft, the house kept it, as it keeps everything.
team.madonna.objective: Let the brave trim their wings, then take the tow and the last word both.
team.madonna.definingEvent: The run-to-the-line lessons: every wing-stripped hero taught the same arithmetic.
team.madonna.carryForward: The office is measuring wing angles now; the crown reads the draft before anyone admits it exists.

team.firenze.summary: The temple had its high priest, and he wore wine-dark. I. Germi turned the low-drag dare into scripture, wings trimmed to nothing, foot buried, and at Italy produced the wing-stripped run the tifosi sang about all campaign. F. Elssler played cold accountant to his own teammate's theatre, riding shotgun when the temple demanded and harvesting what the spectacle wasted. The FIRENZE 500 carried two religions all season, and the FIRENZE 99 V12 sang for both. Then the scrutineers were seen with tape measures, and the house discovered its church had a building inspector.
team.firenze.objective: Make the temples Firenze's parish and let the ice bank what the opera spends.
team.firenze.definingEvent: Italy: Germi's wing-stripped temple run, the drive the tifosi turned into hymn.
team.firenze.carryForward: The religion may be regulated; the house files both pride and alarm, and keeps singing.

team.millions.summary: Noise, the chrome garage decided, was a form of downforce. N. Jones thrived in the slipstream season like no one else, wrestling the MILLIONS 189 into every final-tow duel the tannoy could scream through, the #5 in the middle of the year's first three-abreast finish at France. G. Alberti watched his teammate's theatre with a champion's cool eye and banked the quieter arithmetic. The DICK MD V10's thirst was, for once, somebody else's problem: the tow is free. But the man who reads rulebooks brought worrying news from the scrutineering bay: the office had started measuring wing angles.
team.millions.objective: Win the tow duels, own the three-abreast photographs, and read the wing draft first.
team.millions.definingEvent: France: Jones three-abreast at the festival of the tow, the tannoy hoarse by the flag.
team.millions.carryForward: The rulebook reader is re-employed on the wing draft, and the theatre eyes the building code.

team.bestowal.summary: The cathedral lodged its counter-argument at full wing. While the paddock shaved downforce and prayed, the given house insisted raw cathedral power still beat the tow, and made West Germany the season's rebuttal: the BESTOWAL 167 howling through the forest on the sealed VAPOR DN's brute authority while the trimmed cars danced and scattered. M. Blume's metronome and A. Picos's warm Sundays kept the marble ledger correct. The house watched the drag religion with patrician distaste: bravery is what the underfunded call setup. And when the office began measuring wing angles, the cathedral permitted itself a small, correct smile.
team.bestowal.objective: Prove full-wing power still beats the tow, with West Germany as the standing rebuttal.
team.bestowal.definingEvent: The cathedral counter-argument: full wing through the forest while the trimmed cars danced.
team.bestowal.carryForward: The wing draft finds the house already compliant, and the cathedral enjoys being right in writing.

team.blanche.summary: The whitecoats watched the whole grid shave its wings and reached for the notebook. The tow was a stain like any other, to be studied, logged, and removed, and the BLANCHE 582 ran the temple season on discipline: never the bravest down the straight, always the first still pointed correctly at the corner. J. Herbin's metronome ticked through the drag men's carnival, P. White believed in clean air, and the DELTA 103 V10's honesty looked wiser every Sunday. The whispered wing draft was read in the white garage, logged, and filed under good news.
team.blanche.objective: Solve the tow on paper, keep the discipline, and file the coming regulation under good news.
team.blanche.definingEvent: The wing draft whispered, and the whitecoats the only garage not pretending not to hear.
team.blanche.carryForward: A regulation the white car was practically born to obey, and a door argument that keeps sharpening.

team.tyrant.summary: The tow, at terminal speed, into a braking zone: the boot camp considered the temple season a gift. G. Gould treated the wing-stripped TYRANT 548 as a longer, faster dare, the cruellest brakes in LEVEL B holding heartbeats past the point the drag men lifted, and M. Hamano's crossover ice kept the gunmetal arithmetic exact. The LIZZIE 24 V8 ran light and eager where the monsters ran hot. The season's frights, all walked away from, were studied below decks with professional interest: other people's limits, mapped. The office's tape measures were laughed at. You cannot regulate nerve. Probably.
team.tyrant.objective: Turn the tow into a longer dare and map everyone else's limits while doing it.
team.tyrant.definingEvent: Gould's tow into the braking zone: the season's latest lift, held a heartbeat too long.
team.tyrant.carryForward: The office wants to regulate nerve, and the boot camp is laughing, for now.

team.losel.summary: Finally, a fashion Losel could measure. The drag winter was the wind-tunnel house's native season: wing angles, drag figures, terminal speeds, all of it graphable, and the LOSEL 125's shaved setups were computed rather than dared. E. Pacheco drove the numbers with cold precision, W. Dehehe's blue needle steady, the VAPOR DNPQ V8's openable honesty keeping the allocations inside the spreadsheet. The year's delicious moment came in the scrutineering bay: the office's tape measures, and the data room recognising its own handwriting in the whispered draft. The sport was becoming calculable. Losel had waited years.
team.losel.objective: Compute the tow instead of daring it, and watch the sport become calculable.
team.losel.definingEvent: The wing draft, whispered, in handwriting the data room recognises as its own.
team.losel.carryForward: The sport edges toward the spreadsheet, and the wind tunnel feels the weather changing.

team.may.summary: The tow costs nothing, which made it the season's most May upgrade. The little green car spent the temple season glued to grandee gearboxes down the straights, G. Turner riding every available slipstream and waving at its paymaster on the way past, the MAY 555 suddenly trading above its station on the fast rounds. The LORRY 32 V8, freight-engineering with a racing number, outlasted the trimmed machinery as it outlasts everything. The leaf-green banner kept flying for the little teams: here was proof the new era still had free lunches for garages quick enough to find the draught.
team.may.objective: Ride every free lunch in the draught and keep the banner flying above its station.
team.may.definingEvent: The green car towed past a grandee, waving at the tow's paymaster on the way by.
team.may.carryForward: The spring has learned the slipstream, and the wing draft is priced with genuine concern.

team.bullets.summary: The lock-up watched the temples through the fence, then climbed the fence. The featherweight BULLETS 560 was the tow's cheapest bandit, light enough to slingshot past machinery with three times the budget, and G. Ceara spent the season turning other people's draughts into the #17's private escalator. The LIZZIE 24 V8, leaned on as ever, mostly survived. The garage's eyes kept drifting to the works thunder across the paddock: the temple season proved the chassis belonged at the front; everyone could see what was missing. The dangerous sentence was now said in the lock-up itself.
team.bullets.objective: Bandit the tow, prove the chassis belongs at the front, and covet the works thunder.
team.bullets.definingEvent: The featherweight slingshot: the tow's cheapest bandit, collecting grandees.
team.bullets.carryForward: The dangerous sentence is now said indoors: imagine that chassis with a real engine.

team.dardan.summary: The walled streets do not do slipstreams, and the clan spent the season insisting the year's religion was a heresy. At the walled rounds the DARDAN 700 ran its own counter-temple, no tow, only knives, K. Alfven's ice-over-oxblood hands banking what the fights spared, E. Bellini's temper reading every headline the sister car earned. The VAPOR DNPQ V8 gave its documented honesty to a paddock no longer reading documents. The grudge was not cooling. It was being fed, tow by tow, headline by headline, and the clan's silence had begun to sound like a countdown.
team.dardan.objective: Hold the counter-temple at the walled rounds and feed the grudge on the sister car's headlines.
team.dardan.definingEvent: The walled rounds run as counter-temple: no tow, only knives.
team.dardan.carryForward: A silence that has begun to sound like a countdown, and a temper reading the front pages.

team.linden.summary: Two hundred. The number had been chalked on the garage wall since winter, and in the season's closing rounds the oldest team-and-driver bond in racing reached its double century, every start of it in Linden green. The whole circus, grandees included, stood for the old car, and M. Moreau acknowledged the ovation once, properly, then went back to work. The LINDEN LN198 finished the temple season as it finishes all seasons, the LORRY 32 V8 seasoned past breaking. In the briefing room, the elder's question about speed and circuits kept being minuted. The tree kept everything.
team.linden.objective: Reach the double century in green, accept the ovation once, and keep the minutes.
team.linden.definingEvent: Start two hundred: the double century, all in Linden green, the circus on its feet.
team.linden.carryForward: The elder now belongs to the whole paddock, and the briefing-room minutes keep growing.

team.minarae.summary: The canyons do not tow, but the straights between them do, and the on-ramp added a subject to its curriculum. B. Miller's canyon craft held, brake-late and elbows-out, while J. Nono learned the draught, the MINARAE 594's honest bones a fine classroom for the slipstream's arithmetic. The SEGA SG1000 V8 could not teach thunder; it could teach timing, and the tow is mostly timing. The scouts' notebooks acquired a new line: graduates arrive able to race in company, exactly what the front of the grid was about to need.
team.minarae.objective: Add the draught to the curriculum and keep the canyon craft sharp.
team.minarae.definingEvent: Nono's graduation in the tow: the on-ramp's syllabus, updated for the slipstream era.
team.minarae.carryForward: Graduates who can brake late and tow both, and a seller's market forming.

team.rigel.summary: The museum remembered the tow from the first time it was law. The temple season sent the lifers to the archive, and out came the old slipstream notes, yellowed, beautiful, and suddenly useful: the RIGEL 3000's bones had been set up for draughts when half the grid's engineers were boys. R. Cotman drove the remembered lines faithfully, T. Chardin carried the courtly decay with new relevance, and one tow-assisted grid slot was framed on the wall beside the older miracles. The LORRY 32 V8 kept its superstitions. The star, the museum noted with satisfaction, had seen all of this before.
team.rigel.objective: Teach the tow from the archive and frame one more miracle beside the old ones.
team.rigel.definingEvent: The yellowed slipstream notes, back in service after a generation in the drawer.
team.rigel.carryForward: The ghost owns a subject now, and the museum's memory has become a setup sheet.

team.comet.summary: Free speed is the only kind the spares shelf can afford, and the tow made the matte-black car briefly rich. E. Tornio said nothing and drove everything, the COMET 323 slingshotting out of grandee draughts with the light LIZZIE 24 V8 singing, two Saturdays of flyers that made the timing screens remember. Sundays returned the usual account, the reliability ledger always due, but the temple season gave the romantics something rare: a highlight reel that needed updating twice. The quarrel with Cool over who peaked hardest acquired new evidence, hotly disputed over the shared awning.
team.comet.objective: Get rich on free speed and update the reel twice before the account comes due.
team.comet.definingEvent: The slingshot flyers: two Saturdays of the old light, tow-assisted and glorious.
team.comet.carryForward: The reel has new frames, the quarrel has new evidence, and the schedule still holds.

team.orchis.summary: The greenhouse read the tow as weather, and it worked. C. Tegner, locked out of the horsepower argument, treated the slipstream like a cloud to be timed: the ORCHIS 056 arriving in other cars' draughts at exactly the moment the drag men lifted, the pale flower collecting positions on the run to the line. The MISFIRE 50 V8 still sulked in the heat and sang in the cool, and the hybrid craft, sky-reading and tow-timing, produced the glasshouse's best finishing rate in years. The frost gambler had found his science: meteorology at two hundred miles an hour.
team.orchis.objective: Time the tow like a weather window and build the hybrid craft.
team.orchis.definingEvent: The draught caught like a cloud: positions collected on the run to the line.
team.orchis.carryForward: A science nobody else practises, and the greenhouse's best ledger in years.

team.zeroforce.summary: Even the draft could not lift the 231 far, but far enough to matter at this address. The free speed reached the end of the road like a rumour, and P. Klinger learned to ride whatever draught wandered past the ZEROFORCE 231, the tired LORRY 32 V8 flattered by the company. One Sunday the blacked-out car finished in the same train as the midfield, separated from the brave men's argument by metres rather than minutes, and the chalk wall recorded it as a festival. The wing draft, when whispered, was noted with the crew's usual verdict: unprintable.
team.zeroforce.objective: Ride whatever draught wanders past and record the inches on the chalk wall.
team.zeroforce.definingEvent: The same-train finish: the midfield reached by metres rather than minutes.
team.zeroforce.carryForward: The chalk wall has a new column, and the crew's opinion of regulations remains unprintable.

team.joke.summary: The sandbagger found a new prop. L. Dufay spent the temple season dawdling a second off the pace on Saturdays, then appearing, uninvited, in the middle of every three-abreast photograph the year produced, the JOKE 777's POND V8 towed into company it had no business keeping. The big board had stopped scratching the #16 off, which spoiled half the fun. The wing draft, when whispered, was treated as a review of the act: if the sport was going to regulate straight-line bravery, the carnival intended to be mentioned in the preamble.
team.joke.objective: Photobomb every three-abreast finish and get the act into the regulation's preamble.
team.joke.definingEvent: The #16 in the year's three-abreast photographs, uninvited, unpunished, unmissable.
team.joke.carryForward: The establishment no longer scratches the #16 off, and the clown considers it a mixed review.

team.lares.summary: A gentleman does not draft; he accompanies. The temple season's dares and tow duels were watched from the warm garage with polite interest, and P. Arai went on growing the finishing record the old way: the stout LARES 92 arriving lap after lap, the RAM V12's geological beat unimpressed by the drag men's arithmetic. The season's frights, every one walked away from, were noted at the hearth with gratitude rather than excitement. Points came, as they come, when the brave men's setups unravelled. The lamp stayed lit. The household had seen fashions before. Fashions pass. Finishers remain.
team.lares.objective: Accompany the tow season without joining it, and grow the record on the unravelling.
team.lares.definingEvent: The record growing through the drag men's carnival, one unbroken lap at a time.
team.lares.carryForward: Fashions pass, finishers remain, and the hearth has seen enough fashions to know.

team.feet.summary: The grafters found the one upgrade priced at zero and invoiced it weekly. J. Rampal rode every draught that wandered past Old Boots and then out-braked its owner at the end of the straight, the FEET 13 suddenly arriving in arguments its stopwatch had no ticket for. The YOUGEN V10, hand-fettled and honest, ran the full season without once asking for anything the garage could not afford. The year's frights were watched as working men watch dandies: courage is a trade here, not a costume. The sweat ledger balanced.
team.feet.objective: Invoice the free speed weekly and keep the sweat ledger balanced.
team.feet.definingEvent: Rampal riding the draught past its owner and out-braking him at the end.
team.feet.carryForward: The tow is logged as income, and the paddock's accountants have started noticing.

team.serga.summary: Foreign country, the tow, and the mountain man went and colonised it. E. Sambena learned the slipstream the way he learned welding: wrong once, then never again, and by mid-season the SERGA 1000 was slingshotting out of draughts the mountain engine had no right to be near, the SC3000 F12 eager once warmed and occasionally opinionated about its oil. The free speed suited a garage that could afford nothing else. The countdown beneath the spares shelf ticked on, unacknowledged. Pride goes faster with a tow; the mountain man would not say so, but his right foot knew.
team.serga.objective: Colonise the tow, keep the grey car arriving, and never admit the countdown exists.
team.serga.definingEvent: The slipstream learned wrong once, then never again: the mountain man's newest trade.
team.serga.carryForward: Pride goes faster with a tow, the shelf goes barer by the month, and neither is discussed.

team.cool.summary: Best seats in the house: the deckchairs, the France straight, three abreast at the line. The temple season was the calmest garage's favourite kind of year, theatre everywhere, stakes nowhere, and A. Delvaux composed his love letters to the hairpins while the drag men dared each other past. The COOL 05's CORSE V8 sulked in its electrics on schedule and ran sweetly off it. A top-ten arrived on a tow the garage swears it did not arrange, and was toasted at length. The quarrel with Comet over who peaked hardest now required a photographic appendix, hotly disputed from neighbouring deckchairs.
team.cool.objective: Enjoy the theatre, accept the weather, and dispute the photographic evidence.
team.cool.definingEvent: The France three-abreast watched from the deckchairs and toasted like a title.
team.cool.carryForward: The quarrel has a photographic appendix, and both crews claim the better tow story.

team.moon.summary: The orbit met the draft and found it good. The temple season's slipstreams reached even the far side, and K. Yepes discovered the tow as a form of company: the MOON 292 finishing, twice, in the same train as cars that usually lapped him, the RAM V12's overbuilt patience flattered by the draught. The logbook's new line read: company, briefly. The moonshots stayed unfired; a borrowed tow is a gift, not a strategy, and the far side distrusts gifts. The gap to the pack shrank by another honest degree. The telescope stayed pointed up, as it does.
team.moon.objective: Accept the tow's company, log the gift, and keep the orbit honest.
team.moon.definingEvent: Twice in the same train as the cars that lap him: company, briefly.
team.moon.carryForward: The logbook has a line called company, and the orbit keeps its slow rise.

team.iris.summary: The jewel house trimmed its jewel, carefully. The drag winter asked the IRIS 717 a new question, and the white-glove garage answered with jeweller's precision: wings shaved to the exact angle the PRISM 90 V10 could carry, the violet car knifing the sweepers wing-stripped and arriving in the tow duels the crown hunt required. B. Salgado drove the trimmed configuration like a man who had rehearsed it on paper, because he had. The honest gap to the PALM 190 remained, but the temple season closed it a fraction, and fractions are the cathedral's whole trade.
team.iris.objective: Trim the jewel to the exact angle the hunt requires and close the gap a fraction.
team.iris.definingEvent: The wing-stripped violet car through the sweepers: jewellery, shaved.
team.iris.carryForward: The gap closed a fraction, and the cathedral trades in fractions.

team.azalea.summary: The last car to commit, the first to arrive. The temple season's tow duels were decided in the final hundred metres, and M. Larssen's glacial timing owned those metres all year: the AZALEA 808 leaving its slingshot later than anyone dared and arriving sooner, the BLOOM 88 V8's held breath released at exactly the decisive instant. The sister war ran beneath it all, Iris shaving in public, Azalea collecting in private. The frost does not duel. The frost waits for the run to the line, and the run to the line, this season, belonged to the patient.
team.azalea.objective: Own the final hundred metres of every tow duel worth winning.
team.azalea.definingEvent: The late slingshot: last to commit, first to arrive, all season.
team.azalea.carryForward: The loud sister shaves in public; the cold one collects in private, and prefers it.
